title: Lower ReadWrite Ports during LowerTypes
body:
Fixes #480.

Add lowering of FIRRTL Dialect memories with readwrite ports during the LowerTypes pass. This is accomplished by splitting each readwrite port into one read and one write port. The lowering of aggregate memories into ground type memories proceeds unaffected.

### Example

Starting with the following readwrite memory:

```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input clock: Clock
    input rwEn: UInt<1>
    input rwMode: UInt<1>
    input rwAddr: UInt<4>
    input rwMask: UInt<1>
    input rwDataIn: UInt<8>
    output rwDataOut: UInt<8>

    mem memory:
      data-type => UInt<8>
      depth => 16
      readwriter => rw
      read-latency => 0
      write-latency => 1
      read-under-write => undefined

    memory.rw.clk <= clock
    memory.rw.en <= rwEn
    memory.rw.addr <= rwAddr
    memory.rw.wmode <= rwMode
    memory.rw.wmask <= rwMask
    memory.rw.wdata <= rwDataIn
    rwDataOut <= memory.rw.rdata
```

This is now lowered into the following Verilog:

```verilog
module Foo(
  input        clock, rwEn, rwMode,
  input  [3:0] rwAddr,
  input        rwMask,
  input  [7:0] rwDataIn,
  output [7:0] rwDataOut);

  reg  [7:0] memory[15:0];	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire [3:0] memory_rw_r_addr;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire [7:0] memory_rw_r_data;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire [3:0] memory_rw_w_addr;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire       memory_rw_w_en;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire       memory_rw_w_clk;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire [7:0] memory_rw_w_data;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire       memory_rw_w_mask;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire [3:0] memory_rw_addr;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire       memory_rw_en;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire       memory_rw_wmode;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire       memory_rw_clk;	// Foo.fir:11:5

  assign memory_rw_r_data = memory[memory_rw_r_addr];	// Foo.fir:11:5
  wire _T = memory_rw_w_clk;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  always_ff @(posedge _T) begin	// Foo.fir:11:5
    if (memory_rw_w_en & memory_rw_w_mask)	// Foo.fir:11:5
      memory[memory_rw_w_addr] <= memory_rw_w_data;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  end // always_ff @(posedge)
  `ifndef SYNTHESIS	// Foo.fir:11:5
    initial begin	// Foo.fir:11:5
      `INIT_RANDOM_PROLOG_	// Foo.fir:11:5
      `ifdef RANDOMIZE_MEM_INIT	// Foo.fir:11:5
        integer memory_initvar;
        for (memory_initvar = 0; memory_initvar < 16; memory_initvar = memory_initvar+1)
          memory[memory_initvar] = `RANDOM;	// Foo.fir:11:5
      `endif
    end // initial
  `endif
  wire [3:0] _T_0 = memory_rw_addr;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  assign memory_rw_r_addr = _T_0;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  assign memory_rw_w_addr = _T_0;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  assign memory_rw_w_en = memory_rw_en & memory_rw_wmode;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  assign memory_rw_w_clk = memory_rw_clk;	// Foo.fir:11:5
  assign memory_rw_clk = clock;	// Foo.fir:19:19
  assign memory_rw_en = rwEn;	// Foo.fir:20:18
  assign memory_rw_addr = rwAddr;	// Foo.fir:21:20
  assign memory_rw_wmode = rwMode;	// Foo.fir:22:21
  assign memory_rw_w_mask = rwMask;	// Foo.fir:23:21
  assign memory_rw_w_data = rwDataIn;	// Foo.fir:24:21
  assign rwDataOut = memory_rw_r_data;	// Foo.fir:2:3
endmodule
```

### TODO

- [x] Remove debug prints
- [x] Add test of ground type and aggregate with readwrite port
- [x] Fix problem where new read and write ports collide with existing ports (e.g., for a memory with readwrite port `rw` and read port `rw_r`)
